summary: The CI job built CPython successfully (0 errors) but two tests in test_tcl failed. Both failures show that when Tcl 9.0.3 evaluates a string containing surrogate pairs (\ud83d\udcbb), the _tkinter module returns the raw surrogate pair instead of recombining it into the proper Unicode scalar U+1F4BB (💻), causing assertEqual mismatches.
root_cause: test_eval_surrogates_in_result and test_evalfile_surrogates_in_result both fail because tcl.eval() returns '<\ud83d\udcbb>' (surrogate pair preserved) instead of the expected '<\U0001f4bb>' (recombined emoji). This indicates the _tkinter string-conversion code does not recombine UTF-16 surrogate pairs when decoding results from Tcl 9.0.3, whose internal Unicode model differs from earlier Tcl 8.x.

evidence.reason: Confirms the only failing test module is test_tcl, with exactly 2 failures.
Shows that Tcl 9.0.3 is the runtime, which changed its Unicode model relative to Tcl 8.x and may return characters differently from surrogate-encoded inputs.
The test asserts that a surrogate pair input is returned as a recombined Unicode scalar; this expectation is the one that fails.
The actual return value from tcl.eval still contains the raw surrogate pair instead of the recombined character U+1F4BB, directly showing the mismatch.
Final summary confirms test_tcl is the sole failing test and the cause of the FAILURE exit code.

suggested_fix: Investigate the _tkinter C extension's string decoding path (Modules/_tkinter.c) for how Tcl string objects are converted to Python str when Tcl 9.0 is in use; add logic to recombine UTF-16 surrogate pairs (\ud800-\udfff sequences) into their corresponding Unicode scalar values before returning the Python string, or update the test expectations to reflect Tcl 9.0's actual surrogate handling.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
